title: Establish HTML packing list template
story: As a Dazzling Getaways client, I want a consistently styled packing list page so that every destination guide feels like it came from the same trusted source.
acceptance_criteria: Given the template HTML, when rendered in a browser, then it has a DG logo/header, teal section header bars, yellow PRO TIP callouts, and checkbox-style list items matching the PDF visual language
Given a 375px mobile viewport, when the page loads, then all sections are readable without horizontal scrolling and no content is clipped

Given any of the 4 PDFs used as source, when the template is filled in, then all section types (Documents, Anti-Theft, Tech, Clothing, Toiletries, Hacks, Misc) can be represented without structural changes
notes: Template should be established by building the first guide (Caribbean or whichever is prioritized first), then reused for the remaining three.

title: Caribbean Cruise packing list HTML
story: As a client booked on a Caribbean cruise, I want an HTML packing list so that I can access Kasey's checklist on any device without opening a PDF.
acceptance_criteria: Given the source PDF, when content is extracted, then all 7 sections are present: Clothing & Shoes, Travel Documents & Money, Tech & Electronics, Beach & Pool, Toiletries & Health, Misc Essentials, Genius Cabin Hacks & Cruise Tips
Given the PRO TIP callouts in the PDF, when rendered in HTML, then all 3 tips appear in styled callout blocks with the correct text
Given Kasey's contact info in the PDF footer, when rendered in HTML, then kasey@dazzlinggetaways.com and 309.306.1633 are present
File saved to guides/Caribbean Cruise Packing List/DG_Caribbean_Cruise_Packing_List.html
notes: Source: DG Caribbean Cruise Packing List.pdf. Unique to this guide: cabin hacks section (magnetic hooks, SeaPass lanyard, power strip), beach & pool as its own section.

title: European Trip packing list HTML
story: As a client traveling to Europe, I want an HTML packing list so that I can reference Kasey's checklist including the anti-theft and plug adapter guidance specific to European travel.
acceptance_criteria: Given the source PDF, when content is extracted, then all 7 sections are present: Travel Documents & Money, Anti-Theft Essentials, Tech & Electronics, Misc Essentials, Clothing & Shoes, Toiletries & Health, Genius Travel Hacks & Europe Tips
Given the 3 PRO TIP callouts and 1 featured tip ('My #1 Europe Item'), when rendered, then all appear in styled callout blocks
Given ETIAS authorization is listed in the PDF, when rendered in HTML, then it appears in the Travel Documents section
File saved to guides/European Trip Packing List/DG_European_Trip_Packing_List.html
notes: Source: DG_European_Trip_Packing_List.pdf. Unique: ETIAS item, cobblestone clothing guidance, city-specific anti-theft focus (no waterproof pouch). Subtitle: 'cobblestones, cafés, and everything in between'.

title: Croatia packing list HTML
story: As a client traveling to Croatia, I want an HTML packing list so that I have island-hopping and boat-day specific guidance in an easy-to-access format.
acceptance_criteria: Given the source PDF, when content is extracted, then all 7 sections are present including Croatia-specific items: underwater phone case, quick-dry beach towel, snorkel gear, water shoes with 'rocky beaches are common in Croatia!' note
Given the plug adapter callout, when rendered, then it reads 'Type C/F for Croatia'
Given the currency note, when rendered, then it reads 'Croatia uses the euro' in the Travel Documents PRO TIP
File saved to guides/Croatia Packing List/DG_Croatia_Packing_List.html
notes: Source: DG_Croatia_Packing_List.pdf. Shares ~80% structure with Greece (DG-005) — use Greece as a diff check to catch any missed Croatia-specific text.

title: Greece packing list HTML
story: As a client traveling to Greece, I want an HTML packing list so that I have Aegean island-specific guidance that's distinct from the Croatia list even where structure overlaps.
acceptance_criteria: Given the source PDF, when content is extracted, then destination-specific text reads 'Greece' not 'Croatia' throughout (water shoes note, currency note, island tips header)
Given the plug adapter callout, when rendered, then it reads 'Type C/F for Greece'
Given the subtitle in the PDF, when rendered, then the subtitle reads 'sun-soaked islands, turquoise water, and Aegean magic' exactly
File saved to guides/Greece Packing List/DG_Greece_Packing_List.html
notes: Source: DG_Greece_Packing_List.pdf. Nearly identical to Croatia — key differences: 'Greece' vs 'Croatia' in destination text, subtitle wording, section header ('Aegean magic' vs 'Adriatic coastal magic').

title: Register Caribbean Cruise packing list in build.mjs
story: As a site visitor, I want the Caribbean Cruise packing list accessible at a clean URL so that Kasey can send a direct link to clients.
acceptance_criteria: Given an entry added to the guides array in scripts/build.mjs with path 'caribbean-cruise-packing-list', when npm run build runs, then no errors are thrown
Given the build completes, then dist/caribbean-cruise-packing-list/index.html exists
Given the URL /caribbean-cruise-packing-list/, when visited on the live site, then the Caribbean Cruise packing list loads
notes: Source folder: guides/Caribbean Cruise Packing List/ | File: DG_Caribbean_Cruise_Packing_List.html | URL: /caribbean-cruise-packing-list/

title: Add interactive checkbox state to packing list template
story: As a client using the packing list on my phone, I want to check items off in my browser so that I can track what I've packed without printing anything.
acceptance_criteria: Given a client checks an item, when they reload the page on the same device and browser, then the checked state is preserved via localStorage
Given JavaScript is disabled, when the page loads, then all content is still readable and no layout is broken (progressive enhancement)
Given a client wants to reset, when they use a visible 'Clear all' control, then all checkboxes return to unchecked
notes: CONDITIONAL — implement only if interactive checkboxes are approved. Decision pending.
